title: Doctor Login Token
description: The canonical shape of a DoctorLoginTokens record — docs/33-DOMAIN-MODEL.md §7.1, docs/50-PHASE-3-TECHNICAL-PLAN.md §5.4, ADR-017. The single-use artifact that resolves a doctor's registered email to a doctor_id and, once consumed, a Doctor Session — structurally identical in mechanism to login-token.schema.json, stored in its own DoctorLoginTokens sheet, never LoginTokens. Only the token's hash is ever part of this shape — the raw token itself is never stored, never part of this schema, and exists only transiently in memory between generation and delivery. See doctor-login-token.md for the hashing rationale (shared, unmodified, with login-token.schema.json's own) and sheet-isolation note.

token_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest (64 hex characters) of the raw, single-use doctor login token. The raw token is never stored anywhere, including here — this field exists specifically so a stolen datastore export can never be used to authenticate (ADR-010). Computed by the same, unmodified foundationHashLoginToken_() login-token.schema.json's own token_hash field uses.

doctor_id.description: The Doctor Identity this token, once consumed, resolves to (docs/33 §7.1, ADR-017). Set once at issuance, never modified. Never patient_id — a permanently distinct value space.

issued_at.description: The instant this token was generated. Set once, never modified.

expires_at.description: The instant this token becomes permanently invalid, regardless of use. No renewal — a caller presenting an expired token must request a new one, mirroring login-token.schema.json's own rule.

used_at.description: Empty string until this single-use token is consumed exactly once; a non-empty value is the ISO 8601 instant it was consumed. Deliberately NOT format:"date-time"-constrained at the schema level — an empty string is the documented, intentional "not yet used" sentinel, the same convention login-token.schema.json's own used_at field already establishes. See doctor-login-token.md.
